title: Panzanella
subtitle: Tuscan bread and tomato salad with basil and red wine vinegar.
excerpt: Stale bread torn into chunks and soaked in the juices of ripe tomatoes, dressed with red wine vinegar, olive oil and basil. A summer salad that is better made ahead. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Panzanella. The Tuscan bread and tomato salad is documented in Italian cookbooks from the 16th century, originally made with onion rather than tomato (tomatoes arrived later). The modern tomato-and-bread version appears throughout 20th-century Italian cookbooks. The dish belongs specifically to the tradition of cucina povera: using stale bread rather than wasting it.

content.text: Panzanella only works with very ripe tomatoes. Out of season, or with underripe tomatoes, the salad has no juice and no flavour. Use the ripest tomatoes you can find: overripe is better than underripe. Stale bread is correct, fresh bread turns to paste. Leave the assembled salad for 30 minutes at room temperature before serving.

items.name: Tomato
items.prepNote: very ripe, mixed sizes, roughly torn or sliced

items.name: Bread
items.prepNote: stale sourdough or ciabatta, torn into 3 cm chunks

content.text: Tear {{tomato}} of ripe tomatoes into rough pieces and put in a large bowl with any juice. Add {{bread}} of stale bread, {{cucumber}} of chopped cucumber, {{red-onion}} of thinly sliced red onion and {{basil}} of torn basil leaves. Dress with {{olive-oil}} of extra-virgin olive oil, {{red-wine-vinegar}} of red wine vinegar, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Toss well and leave for 30 minutes at room temperature. Taste and adjust the seasoning before serving.

items.fix: Use properly stale bread, ideally a day or two old. Fresh bread disintegrates. Reduce the vinegar slightly if the dressing is very sharp.
items.cause: Bread too fresh, or too much vinegar.

items.fix: The tomato juice is what soaks the bread. Out-of-season tomatoes will never make good panzanella, this is strictly a summer dish.
items.cause: Tomatoes not ripe enough, or not rested long enough.

items.fix: Soak the sliced onion in cold water for 10 minutes then drain; this removes most of the harsh bite.
items.cause: Red onion not soaked before use.

content.text: Panzanella is a Tuscan farmhouse dish invented to use stale bread, not to waste it. The bread absorbs the tomato juices and becomes the best part of the salad. Abroad, the dish is sometimes made with toasted or fried bread, which misses the point: the soaking is everything. Serve in high summer when tomatoes are properly ripe, with nothing on the side.
